title: Unified entropy entanglement
authors: Wenxue Ren, Binghao Li, Ruiqun Niu, Yu Guo, Shuanping Du
abstract: The unified entropy as a promotion of the von Neumann entropy exhibits distinct diversity which contains the Tsallis entropy, the Rényi entropy, the von Neumann entropy as special cases. The unified-($r,t$) entropy entanglement with $0<r<1$ and $0< t\leq 1$ was shown to be an entanglement monotone in literature. In this paper, we explore unified-($q,s$) entropy entanglement with $q>1$ and $qs\geq1$ and show that it is also an entanglement monotone and that both of them are monogamous. Going further, we present two kinds of global multipartite entanglement measures (GlMEMs) based on the unified entropy and each kind has two subclasses which are classified by the parameters $(q,s)$ and $(r,t)$. Consequently, from the view of the complete multipartite entanglement measure theory, we show that one of them is a complete multipartite entanglement monotone and is not only completely monogamous but also tightly completely monogamous, but the other three are even not complete. We also explore the genuine entanglement measures induced by the unified entropy and the relations with the bipartite entanglement and the global entanglement are discussed, respectively.
summary: This paper introduces a generalized framework for entanglement measures based on 'unified entropy,' which encompasses Tsallis and Renyi entropies. It explores the properties of both (q,s) and (r,t) type entanglement, proving their monotonicity and monogamy while identifying specific measures that satisfy tight complete monogamy. The study provides a unified approach to understanding multipartite entanglement distribution.
main_problem: The paper aims to develop and characterize a unified theory of multipartite entanglement measures using the 'unified entropy' framework, specifically investigating the properties of (q,s) and (r,t) type entropy entanglement.
main_result: The authors prove that (q,s) entropy entanglement is an entanglement monotone and is monogamous, and they identify one specific global multipartite entanglement measure (GlMEM) that is both completely and tightly completely monogamous.
method: The study employs mathematical frameworks involving the unified entropy definition, partition theory (coarsening relations), LOCC analysis, and the use of Schmidt decomposition and operator convexity.
model_or_system: The study focuses on n-partite quantum systems consisting of finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces, specifically analyzing pure and mixed states, including the two-qubit Werner state as a benchmark.
key_observables: Unified entropy entanglement measures (E_{q,s}, E_{r,t}), Global Multipartite Entanglement Measures (GlMEMs), and Genuine Entanglement Measures (GEMs).
important_parameters: Entropy parameters (q, s, r, t) defining different regimes, such as 0 < r < 1 and q > 1, and the dimension of the subsystems.
main_assumptions: The analysis of mixed states assumes the existence of an optimal decomposition via the convex-roof extension.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the unified entropy framework, defines bipartite and multipartite entanglement measures, establishes monotonicity and monogamy properties for different parameter regimes, compares different types of global measures, and provides analytical proofs for hierarchy violations.
why_interesting: This work provides a generalized mathematical framework for quantifying entanglement, which is fundamental for understanding entanglement distribution and monogamy in multipartite quantum networks and many-body systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
